据报道，SpaceX 秘密申请以 1.75T 美元的价格上市

SpaceX confidentially files to go public at $1.75T, reports say

www.theguardian.com 作者 bookofjoe 分数 75 评论 2 2026/04/02 03:19
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 据彭博社和《华尔街日报》报道，SpaceX 已秘密申请在美国股市进行首次公开​​募股。此次 IPO 将成为市场历史上最受关注、估值最高的上市之一。埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的公司已成为太空旅行和卫星通信领域的主导力量，估值可能高达 1.75 兆美元以上。这份机密文件将使监管机构有一段时间在投资者和公众能够查看该公司的财务披露之前进行审查和讨论。 据彭博社报道，IPO 最早可能在 6 月份进行，预计今年将是高价值公开发行的标志性年份。马斯克的竞争对手 OpenAI 也计划在今年晚些时候以极高的估值上市，该公司周二宣布已完成一轮 1220 亿美元的融资，此外人工智能公司 Anthropic 也在准备自己的 IPO。 SpaceX 是马斯克自己的人工智能公司 xAI 的母公司。SpaceX 没有立即回应置评请求。马斯克已经是世界首富，他的财富将通过 IPO 进一步增加，并更接近成为世界上唯一的万亿富翁。在经历了动荡的一年之后，包括监督道奇对联邦政府的内讧、对特斯拉的广泛抗议，以及对他的 Grok 聊天机器人大规模制作未经同意的妇女和儿童色情图片的调查，成功的公开募股将是这位科技大亨的重大胜利。在过去的十年里，SpaceX 已经成长为马斯克庞大的科技帝国中最有价值的企业。它与美国政府签署了广泛的、利润丰厚的国防和太空旅行合同。美国太空计划严重依赖该公司，美国宇航局 (NASA) 的大部分发射都使用 SpaceX 火箭。 除了火箭之外，SpaceX 还通过其子公司 Starlink 发展成为卫星通信大国，该公司是全球最大的卫星运营商之一。据估计，卫星互联网服务将为 SpaceX 带来超过一半的收入，并且还提高了马斯克的地缘政治影响力，乌克兰军方等外国参与者都依赖 Starlink 进行作战行动和规划。 在考虑 IPO 时，SpaceX 还在 2 月份收购了马斯克的 xAI，理由是计划在太空建立太阳能数据中心，这有助于满足人工智能繁荣时期的计算机和能源需求。这家拥有社交媒体平台 X 并开发了 Grok 聊天机器人的人工智能公司在能力上落后于 Anthropic 的 Claude 或 OpenAI 的 ChatGPT 等竞争对手，但此次收购的估值仍高达 2500 亿美元。近几个月来，SpaceX 将重点更多地转向轨道数据中心，并协助 NASA 即将到来的登月任务，而马斯克长期以来誓言在火星上建立文明的计划已不再那么突出。 SpaceX 的财务细节长期以来甚至对大多数投资者来说都是严格保密的，但很可能会在临近 IPO 时披露。股票成本和其他上市信息也将在美国证券交易委员会审查备案后公开。据彭博社报道，该公司正在与包括英国巴克莱银行在内的多家知名国际银行合作，协调 IPO 订单。

Original Summary

  1. SpaceX has confidentially filed for an initial public offering on the US stock market, according to reports from Bloomberg and the Wall Street Journal. The IPO is set to be one of the most closely watched and highly valued listings in market history.Elon Musk’s company, which has become a dominant power in both space travel and satellite communications, could potentially seek a valuation upwards of $1.75tn. The confidential filing will give regulators a period to review and discuss the company’s financial disclosures before investors and the public are able to view them.The IPO could take place as early as June, Bloomberg reported, in what is expected to be a banner year for high-value public offerings. Musk’s rival OpenAI is also planning to go public later this year at an immense valuation, announcing on Tuesday that it had closed a funding round of $122bn, in addition to fellow AI firm Anthropic preparing its own IPO. SpaceX is the parent company of Musk’s own artificial intelligence company, xAI.SpaceX did not immediately respond to a request for comment.Musk, already the world’s richest man, stands to grow his fortune even more from the IPO and get closer to becoming the world’s sole trillionaire. A successful public offering would be a major victory for the tech mogul after a volatile year that included overseeing Doge’s gutting of the federal government, widespread protests against Tesla and investigations into his Grok chatbot mass-producing non-consensual sexualized images of women and children.Over the past decade SpaceX has grown to become the most valuable enterprise in Musk’s sprawling tech empire. It has signed extensive, lucrative contracts with the US government for defense and space travel. The US space program is heavily dependent on the company, with Nasa utilizing SpaceX rockets for the majority of its launches.In addition to its rockets, SpaceX has grown to be a big satellite communications power through its subsidiary Starlink, which ranks among the largest operators of satellites on the planet. The satellite internet service is estimated to bring in over half of SpaceX’s revenue and has also increased Musk’s geopolitical influence, with foreign actors such as the Ukrainian military relying on Starlink for combat operations and planning.As it eyed an IPO, SpaceX also acquired Musk’s xAI in February – citing plans to build solar-powered datacenters in space that could help meet the computer and energy demands of the AI boom. The artificial intelligence firm, which owns the social media platform X and makes the Grok chatbot, has lagged behind the capabilities of rivals such as Anthropic’s Claude or OpenAI’s ChatGPT but was nevertheless valued at $250bn in the acquisition.SpaceX has shifted its focus in recent months more toward orbital datacenters and assisting with Nasa’s forthcoming moon landing missions, while Musk’s long-vowed plans to build a civilization on Mars are no longer as prominent a talking point.Details of SpaceX’s finances, long a tightly held secret even to most investors, are likely to be revealed closer to the IPO. The cost of shares and other information on the listing will also become public after the Securities and Exchange Commission reviews the filing. The company is working with a number of prominent international banks, including Barclays plc in the UK, to coordinate on orders for the IPO, according to Bloomberg.
